title: paspalum nelsonii
intro: Paspalum nelsonii, also known as Nelson's grass, is a type of tropical grass native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin problems due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Paspalum nelsonii include its ability to soothe digestive issues, such as diarrhea and ulcers, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Paspalum nelsonii include teas, infusions, and topical applications, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
